when_to_use: To talk about ability (what you are able to do) or permission (what is allowed).
when_to_use_ar: للتحدث عن القدرة (إيش تقدر تسوي) أو الإذن (إيش مسموح).

the_rule.positive: Subject + can + base verb (NEVER add -s, NEVER add 'to')
the_rule.question: Can + subject + base verb?
the_rule.critical: Can is the same for ALL subjects: I can, he can, she can, they can — NO changes.

pronunciation_note.can_positive: In positive sentences, 'can' is WEAK: /kən/ — sounds like 'kun' (short, unstressed)
pronunciation_note.can_negative: In negative, 'can't' is STRONG: /kænt/ — sounds like 'KANT' (long, stressed)
pronunciation_note.tip: This is how native speakers hear the difference: the vowel sound, not the 't'.
pronunciation_note.tip_ar: الفرق: can = ضعيفة وسريعة (كَن). can't = قوية وواضحة (كَانت).

common_errors.explanation: NEVER put 'to' after can. Always: can + base verb directly.
Can NEVER gets -s. It stays the same for everyone.
Use 'can't' (one word). 'Cannot' is formal writing only.
After can = base verb. No -s, no -ing.

quick_rule: can + base verb. can't + base verb. Same for ALL subjects. Never add -s, never add 'to'.
quick_rule_ar: can + فعل أساسي. can't + فعل أساسي. نفس الشي لكل الضمائر. بدون s، بدون to.
connect_to_speaking: Talk about yourself: 'I can speak Arabic. I can cook. I can't swim yet. I can't speak English fast — but I'm learning!'
